location.office_time_required: up to 10% travel required; show up visibly across global offices

level_signals: 12+ years in business operations, strategic operations, or Chief of Staff roles
Experience building and leading high-performing teams
Works directly with Co-CEOs and senior leadership team
Owns the whole operating architecture of the company
Comp range $224K–$336K base consistent with VP/Senior Director at a post-IPO SaaS company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years in business operations, strategic operations, or Chief of Staff roles at high-growth technology companies — ideally with experience across both hypergrowth and post-IPO phases
Proven track record of building and running a company-wide operating system — OKRs, planning, performance management — not just participating in one someone else designed
Experience leading through major organizational inflection points: rapid scaling, go-public events, integrations, or significant structural change
Demonstrated ability to work directly with and influence CEOs and senior leadership teams without relying on positional authority — through clarity, credibility, and quality of thinking
Experience building and leading high-performing teams; a developer of people, not just a driver of output
Financial fluency is non-negotiable: comfort with LRP/annual planning cycles, unit economics, and working alongside a CFO and finance org as a true peer — not just a consumer of their outputs
Exceptional communicator: writes with precision, speaks with confidence, and can hold a room with a Board narrative or an all-hands equally well
Moves with urgency: comfortable in short iterative cycles, makes decisions with incomplete information, creates momentum where others create decks
Operationally obsessed: you notice inefficiency like a splinter; you can't leave it alone until it's fixed

AI-native operator: you understand how to design and leverage AI systems to automate reporting, surface real-time visibility into KPIs and OKRs, and drive company-wide efficiency — and you're resourceful enough to go find the source data yourself rather than waiting for someone to hand it to you
Data-driven and influential: you don't just analyze — you use data as a lever; your insights shape decisions, change minds, and move the org toward better outcomes
A natural integrator: builds trust with functional leaders, navigates tension without drama, keeps the company moving without becoming the bottleneck

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in management consulting or investment banking a plus; operators who built their skills in the trenches are equally valued
SaaS, B2B software, or marketing technology experience preferred

explicitly_excluded: This is not a chief of staff role that begins and ends with the CEO's calendar. It is not a program manager who tracks work other people own. And it is not a strategic advisor who synthesizes without executing.

scope.surface: Company-wide operating architecture — strategy setting, OKRs, planning, cross-functional execution, leadership team cadences, Board management, and AI adoption
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / B2C CRM (autonomous B2C CRM platform)
core_responsibilities: Lead annual and multi-year operating planning process
Own the company-wide OKR process end to end
Define and manage company KPIs across functions; run performance reviews and operational audits
Drive automation of KPIs and OKR results using AI tools and internal agents
Own the management operating system: meetings, cadences, decision forums
Lead and/or support highest-priority cross-functional initiatives
Design and run operating cadences for Co-CEOs and senior leadership team
Support preparation of Board meeting materials and manage Board portal
Champion AI adoption across the company
Show up visibly across Klaviyo — all-hands, team meetings, KRGs, global offices

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($224K–$336K base) and competitive
Role has clear, substantive accountability — 'owns the whole operating architecture'
Explicit 'What This Role Is Not' section sets honest expectations
Company at a genuine inflection point with clear strategic mandate
Annual cash bonus and equity mentioned in total comp package
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information provided — unclear for international candidates
Extremely broad scope (entire operating architecture) with unclear team size — could mean very lean support structure
12+ year minimum and financial fluency described as 'non-negotiable' narrows the pool significantly
Role requires high travel visibility across global offices despite no explicit office policy stated
Use of AI screening tool (Covey) disclosed; may affect candidate evaluation

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from scope, reporting relationship to Co-CEOs, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'autonomous B2C CRM company' and 'SaaS' mention)
location.cities_listed (JD references 'global offices' and 'all US locations' for pay but no specific cities tied to this role)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'Base Pay Range For US Locations' covering multiple markets and no office mandate stated for this role)
ambiguities: Location type is ambiguous: the JD mentions showing up in global offices and up to 10% travel but does not specify a primary city or hybrid/remote policy. Treated as hybrid given the expectation of physical presence at various offices.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; marked as 'unclear'.

The 'Background in management consulting or investment banking a plus' is immediately followed by 'operators who built their skills in the trenches are equally valued' — softening what appears to be a nice-to-have.

The 'ideally with experience across both hypergrowth and post-IPO phases' qualifier on the 12+ years requirement is framed as ideal, potentially softening the post-IPO portion, but the 12-year minimum itself is treated as hard.
